name: SMBC
attribution: Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal by Zach Weinersmith

comics.title: Sleep
comics.alt: The real punchline comes later when he marries a person who loves him, has a good boss, is respected by his children, works at a rewarding job for 40 years, travels the world for 25 years, and dies in painless repose, surrounded by loved ones.

comics.title: Block
comics.alt: Technically, this is literary humor.

comics.title: Joke
comics.alt: Do they still do this? When I was a kid, villains were constantly dangling from buildings and being saved.

comics.title: Shenanigans
comics.alt: Based almost entirely on something Tomer Ullman told me, and I hereby nominate him as First Dean of Shenanigans.

comics.title: Connection
comics.alt: All I'm saying is this would be the ultimate proof that God has a dirty mind.

comics.title: Future
comics.alt: I read in an econ book that panel four is a thing that actually happens, and I've been terrified ever since.

comics.title: Peace
comics.alt: Triple the median would of course be 50% more inner peace.

comics.title: Nagel
comics.alt: The best part is that it's included in a book titled Mortal Questions. Also that the original paper came out in 1969.

comics.title: Tragic
comics.alt: Later he's upset that nobody's upset.

comics.title: Meaning
comics.alt: The worst part is the robot makes him use reddit 7 hours a day, because that's what past employees were doing in its training data.

comics.title: Spoon
comics.alt: Ten bucks to any president who does the thing in the second-to-last panel.

comics.title: Kickstarter Launch!
comics.alt: A book 12 years in the making...

comics.title: Copernican
comics.alt: This joke is more for my future audience of robots, but humans are welcome.

comics.title: Set
comics.alt: Why start with a confusing idea like zero, when you can refer to the notion of a something with nothing in it?

comics.title: Quantum
comics.alt: Later they go out for a superposition of chocolate and vanilla ice cream.

comics.title: End
comics.alt: During the book tour of A City on Mars, I did a talk with Randall Munroe in NYC, and he made, impromptu, a better joke than any of us have committed to paper. During a discussion of whether you could eliminate all life on Earth for a reasonable price, a person asked if there might be a way you could specifically annihilate Queens. To which he replied, 'regicide?'

comics.title: Slap
comics.alt: Later it works out because the man is a masochist.

comics.title: Happiness
comics.alt: You're looking at the teeth, but you're missing... the smile!

comics.title: WaaS
comics.alt: Have I mentioned SMBC's ad-free patreon page?

comics.title: Standard
comics.alt: Should this be rated NSFW?
